introduction: The central campfire burns low, barely more than embers. Around it, families have arranged their meager possessions.
You notice signs of struggle—broken tent stakes, trampled ground, bloodstains on the dirt. This camp has seen violence.
A makeshift shrine sits near the fire—small tokens and prayers for the missing. Names are carved into wooden markers: 'Elara', 'Sophie', 'Thomas', 'Anna', and many others.
The fire provides warmth, but it cannot dispel the fear that hangs over this place.
